meta.note: Scaffold deck for the v0.2 pilot. Two questions per archetype so the end-to-end scoring flow ships. The full 95-question set lands in v0.2.1 from the parallel-session build (local_cd5290a6-16d4-4346-b174-bba5dc556a16). Each question is a Likert statement rated 1 to 5. Weights are 1 unless a follow-up calibration says otherwise.

questions.text: I set up small systems that make other people's lives easier before they ask.
I remember small preferences and details about the people I work with.
I think about how today's moves compound over the next three to five years.
I catch patterns across projects that other people treat as unrelated.
Before a decision I want to see the numbers, the source, and the base rate.
I trust a clean model over a story every time.
I get bored quickly when the room is talking about tactics instead of what's possible.
I can hold a picture of the future in enough detail to describe it to others.
I take on the fight for people who cannot advocate for themselves in the room.
I read a mission before I read a business plan.
I reach for a specific story or scene when I want people to actually feel the point.
I edit sentences out loud until they sound the way a human would say them.
I would rather ship something at 80 percent than talk about it at 100 percent.
When a project stalls, I am usually the one who moves it back into motion.
When someone I care about is stuck, I want to teach them the shape of the problem rather than solve it for them.
People come to me for perspective more often than they come for tactics.
